description_long: This is a double-bladed battle axe with a robust, dark metallic head featuring two symmetrical, crescent-shaped blades. The blades have a slightly reflective, steel-gray edge and a matte black central body, giving them a menacing and utilitarian appearance. The handle is long, straight, and appears to be made of a sturdy, dark material, possibly metal or reinforced composite, with a subtle grip section near the base for secure handling. The transition between the handle and the blade is reinforced, suggesting durability and strength for heavy-duty use. The overall design is functional yet imposing, suitable for display, ceremonial, or practical purposes in an urban or garage setting. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, emphasizing a utilitarian aesthetic.
description: A double-bladed, steel-gray and black battle axe with a long, straight, dark handle and robust, symmetrical blades. The design is functional and imposing, with a matte finish and reinforced construction.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the sharp, reflective edges of both crescent-shaped blades and the full length of the dark handle.
description_view_1: A side profile view emphasizes the thinness of the blade and the straightness of the handle, with the blade's edge and central mounting clearly visible.
description_view_2: This view shows the opposite side, again displaying the symmetrical double blades and the reinforced connection between blade and handle.
description_view_3: The axe is angled to show the curvature of the blade edges and the grip section at the base of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handle made of dark composite or metal, with a subtle grip section near the base; no visible curvature or unique styling.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, symmetrical crescent-shaped blades with sharp steel-gray edges and a matte black central body; no beard or poll, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with reinforced transition between blade and handle.
